paragraphs.text:
Notes and insight:
- These counts reflect distinct macroscopic protonation steps (unique HnL → H(n−1)L transitions), deduplicated across measurements at different conditions.
- High counts cluster in polyaza macrocycles and polyaminopolycarboxylate/phosphonate ligands (e.g., DTPA, TTHA, PHOA, TPHA, DTPP), consistent with many protonatable amines and acidic groups. Phytic acid’s eight steps align with multiple phosphate groups contributing to titration behavior.

claims.text: These counts reflect distinct macroscopic protonation steps
deduplicated across measurements at different conditions.
High counts cluster in polyaza macrocycles and polyaminopolycarboxylate/phosphonate ligands
consistent with many protonatable amines and acidic groups.
align with multiple phosphate groups contributing to titration behavior.

paragraphs.text: Source: NIST SRD-46 (ligand_pka_measured joined to ligand_card; grouped by distinct bracket transitions).

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: Step 18 tool: execute_srd46_sql (query against SRD-46 schema)

tool_snippets.snippet:
## execute_srd46_sql - 33 row(s)
**Args:** {"sql_query": "SELECT ... FROM ligand_pka_measured AS p JOIN ligand_card AS lc ..."}

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: FROM ligand_pka_measured AS p JOIN ligand_card AS lc ON lc.ligand_id = p.ligand_id

tool_snippets.snippet:
SELECT 
  CONCAT('ligand_', lc.ligand_id) AS ligand_id,
  ...
FROM ligand_pka_measured AS p
JOIN ligand_card AS lc ON lc.ligand_id = p.ligand_id
## get_table_schema - ligand_pka_measured ... | name | pka_id | ligand_id | bracket_from_state | bracket_to_state | pKa | ...
## get_table_schema - ligand_card ... | name | ligand_id | ligand_name_SRD | ligand_class_name | ...

SELECT ... COUNT(DISTINCT (p.bracket_from_state || '→' || p.bracket_to_state)) AS distinct_pKa_steps
FROM ligand_pka_measured AS p JOIN ligand_card AS lc ...

WHERE p.bracket_from_state IS NOT NULL AND p.bracket_to_state IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY lc.ligand_id, lc.ligand_name_SRD, lc.ligand_class_name
